3.why: We need the size to wire up next pointers.
3.invariant: n is the number of nodes.

4.why: nxt[i] will hold the id node i points to.

5.happening: Loop over every node to set its next pointer.
5.why: Each node needs to know its successor.
5.invariant: Nodes below i are already wired.

6.happening: Node {i} is not the tail — its next is the following node.
6.why: Interior nodes point at the next index.
6.invariant: Only the tail can start a cycle.

6.happening: Node {i} is the tail — its next is the cycle target.
6.why: The tail is where a cycle (if any) links back.
6.invariant: The tail's next is pos, or -1.

7.why: Interior nodes link to their immediate successor.

9.why: pos is the cycle target; -1 means the list simply ends.
9.invariant: A back-edge to pos creates the cycle.

10.why: Both pointers start together at the head.
10.invariant: slow is the tortoise, moving one step at a time.

11.happening: Place fast at the head too.
11.why: fast will move twice as quickly as slow.
11.invariant: fast is the hare, moving two steps at a time.

12.why: No cycle has been detected yet.
12.invariant: found becomes True only if the pointers meet.

13.happening: fast has a node and a next — it can take another double hop.
13.why: Two hops need two edges ahead of fast.
13.invariant: If fast reaches the end, there is no cycle.

13.happening: fast (or its next) fell off the end — no cycle.
13.why: A pointer that reaches null proves the list terminates.

14.why: The hare moves twice as fast as the tortoise.
14.invariant: In a cycle, fast gains one node on slow each step.

15.why: The tortoise advances a single node.
15.invariant: slow trails fast by a growing gap inside a cycle.

16.happening: fast and slow landed on the same node ({fast}) — they've met!
16.why: Two pointers at different speeds can only coincide inside a cycle.
16.invariant: A meeting proves a cycle exists.

16.happening: fast ({fast}) and slow ({slow}) are on different nodes — keep racing.
16.why: No meeting yet; the hare keeps gaining on the tortoise.
16.invariant: If a cycle exists they will eventually coincide.

17.happening: Record that a cycle was found.
17.why: The pointers met, which only happens in a cycle.

18.happening: Stop early — the answer is settled.
18.why: Once a cycle is confirmed there's nothing left to check.
18.invariant: The loop ends with found = True.

19.happening: Return found — whether the list has a cycle.

init.happening: Building the linked list (with any cycle link) and placing both pointers at the head.
init.why: We need the node chain before the race can begin.
init.invariant: slow and fast both start at the head.

loop.happening: Checking whether fast can take another double hop.
loop.why: The race continues while fast has room ahead.
loop.invariant: Reaching the end means no cycle.

move.happening: Advancing the pointers — fast by two, slow by one.
move.why: The speed difference closes any cyclic gap.
move.invariant: fast gains one node on slow per step inside a cycle.

check.happening: Comparing whether fast and slow share a node.
check.why: A collision is the signature of a cycle.

return.happening: Returning whether a cycle was found.
return.why: Either the pointers met or fast reached the end.
